steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The phrase “Including Rasta, Pretty Boy, and Makhulu” provides additional examples of the lions. The em dash after “Makhulu” signals the close of this parenthetical information.

steps.title: Match the punctuation
steps.content: When an appositive is closed with an em dash, it must be opened with a matching em dash: “coalition—information—thrived”. Option A provides the opening em dash, creating symmetry. Options B and D use different punctuation (semicolon and comma) that cannot match a closing em dash, and Option C leaves no opening mark, breaking the paired structure. That matches choice A — A is correct.
